mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, the restrictive monetary stance depresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening residential demand and reduced construction employment, which cascades into lower consumer confidence and reduced discretionary spending. This demand contraction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances following > 10% month-over-month HOUST declines, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market weakness that reduces household income and consumer spending capacity. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 138122.0 (thousands) as of 2007-07-01
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when both UNRATE rises > 0.3pp and PAYEMS declines, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a real-time gauge of manufacturing capacity utilization and economic momentum. Sustained month-over-month declines in INDPRO signal supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 62% of instances following > 0.5% month-over-month INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflect consumer spending behavior and aggregate demand. When RSAFS growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year, it signals weakening consumer demand that constrains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 376051.0 (millions of dollars) as of 2007-07-01
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with consumer spending deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when RSAFS year-over-year growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated FEDFUNDS (> 5.0%) signals the Federal Reserve is in active tightening mode to combat inflation. This dual monetary restriction compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 208.028 and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of 2007-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of instances when both CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as the ultimate demand signal. When annualized real GDP growth falls below 2.0%, it signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth assumptions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11507.9 (billions of chained 2017 dollars) as of 2007-04-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when annualized GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5.0% but rising, it signals the labor market is tightening from an already tight state, which constrains wage growth and corporate profit margins. In this regime, further UNRATE increases produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as margin compression concerns dominate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% AND month-over-month increase > 0.1 percentage points
regime_hint: tight labor market with rising unemployment from low base
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when UNRATE < 5.0% and rises > 0.1pp month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
